subject: [RFC PATCH 35/40] mm: page_alloc: refuse best-effort high-order allocs servable at lower orders

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch prevents best-effort high-order memory allocations from succeeding when a lower order allocation would be sufficient. It detects this pattern by checking if the allocation request has __GFP_NOWARN set and __GFP_NOFAIL not set, and then checks if the tainted pool can plausibly serve a smaller or same order allocation on the caller's retry. If so, it refuses the current attempt instead of escalating. This fix is intended to prevent high-order allocations from succeeding when they have an order-decrement fallback, which would otherwise lead to unnecessary escalation.
